findings.message: Agent does not declare a model tier
Agent has zero example invocations
Uses 'best subtype' and 'clean, well-structured code' — vague without defining what 'best' means or what structure is required
Agent does not declare a model tier; callers cannot determine expected cost or capability tier
Agent has zero example invocations; users have no concrete reference for how to call it
Uses vague quantifiers 'most appropriate layout' and 'optimal layout' without concrete selection criteria
Agent does not declare a model tier
Agent has zero example invocations
Uses 'appropriate layout' and 'effective professional layouts' — 'effective' and 'appropriate' are vague without measurable criteria
Agent does not declare a model tier
Agent has zero example invocations
Agent does not declare a model tier
Agent has zero example invocations
Skill does not declare a `license` field; other skills in the same plugin subtree include license
Skill does not declare a `license` field
`requests>=2.28` uses a floor constraint with no upper bound; a future major version with breaking changes or vulnerabilities could be installed silently
Downloads `https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh` to /tmp/, chmod +x, then executes it — functionally equivalent to curl-pipe-sh; trusts external URL at runtime
Uses sudo with apt-get, dnf, pacman, and zypper to install system packages; grants root privileges to the install script
Appends `export PATH=$HOME/.dotnet:$PATH` to ~/.bashrc, permanently modifying the user's shell PATH across sessions
Frontmatter declares `name: mmx-cli` but the directory is `minimax-multimodal-toolkit`; validate_skills.py hard check requires name to match directory name
Skill is missing both `license` and `metadata` fields; all peer skills in the repository include both
Skill name `mmx-cli` differs from directory name `minimax-multimodal-toolkit`; breaks convention enforced by validate_skills.py and causes skill discovery failures
Runs `pip install reportlab pypdf matplotlib` at runtime without version pins or hash verification; susceptible to supply-chain compromise on dependency update
Launches Playwright/Chromium to render HTML to PDF; headless browsers are a significant attack surface and should be scoped to a sandboxed environment
skills/pptx-generator and plugins/pptx-plugin cover identical PowerPoint generation use cases; both trigger on 'PPT/PPTX/PowerPoint/slide'; users loading both may get ambiguous routing
Frontmatter declares `name: mmx-cli` but the directory is `minimax-multimodal-toolkit`; validate_skills.py hard check requires name to match directory name
